title: Resource Guard with Refresh Token
statement: Implement an authentication system with a refresh token feature using JSON Web Tokens (JWT) in an Express.js server. The server should have a protected endpoint to access a resource. Upon successful login, a JWT token and a refresh token are issued. The user can use the JWT token to access the protected resource once. The refresh token can be used to obtain a new JWT token when the original JWT token expires. Ensure proper validation and handling of expired, invalid, or missing tokens. The server should also enforce a rate limit of 10 requests per minute per user to prevent abuse.
constraints: Use the in-memory array provided for users and tokens
Use the `jsonwebtoken` library for JWT and refresh token management

endpoints.description: Issues a JWT token and a refresh token upon successful login with valid username and password. Expects a JSON payload containing the username and password.
Issues a new JWT token upon successful refresh with a valid refresh token. Expects a JSON payload containing the refresh token.
Returns the protected resource upon successful authentication with a valid JWT token.
